concepts.title: Categories and morphisms
concepts.blurb: A category organizes objects and arrows with associative composition and identities.

concepts.title: Functors and natural transformations
concepts.blurb: Functors map structure between categories, and natural transformations compare functors componentwise.

concepts.title: Yoneda, limits, and adjunctions
concepts.blurb: Yoneda and adjunctions turn universal properties into a computational language used across modern mathematics.

concepts.title: Universal properties and representability
concepts.blurb: A universal property pins an object down as the representing object of a Hom-functor; every standard construction (product, free group, limit, adjoint) is a universal property in disguise.

concepts.title: Monoidal categories
concepts.blurb: A monoidal category has an associative bifunctor and a unit, up to coherent natural isomorphism; it is the setting for tensor products, braidings, and algebraic structures internal to a category.

concepts.title: The Yoneda lemma
concepts.blurb: $\mathrm{Nat}(h_A,F)\cong F(A)$: every natural transformation out of a representable is pinned by a single element, and every object is determined by its functor of points.

concepts.title: Limits and colimits
concepts.blurb: A (co)limit is the universal (co)cone on a diagram; products, equalizers, pullbacks and their duals are all special cases of the same representability problem.

concepts.title: Adjunctions: unit, counit, triangle
concepts.blurb: An adjunction $F\dashv G$ is a natural Hom-isomorphism, equivalently unit/counit satisfying the triangle identities; left adjoints preserve colimits, right adjoints preserve limits.

concepts.title: Monads and their algebras
concepts.blurb: A monad $(T,\mu,\eta)$ is a monoid in endofunctors; Kleisli and Eilenberg–Moore categories recover the adjunction and classify its algebras.

concepts.title: Kan extensions
concepts.blurb: Left and right Kan extensions along a functor extend structure as freely (or cofreely) as possible; Mac Lane's aphorism ‘all concepts are Kan extensions’ is literally true.

concepts.title: 2-categories preview
concepts.blurb: A 2-category has objects, 1-morphisms, and 2-morphisms between 1-morphisms; $\mathsf{Cat}$ is the archetype, with functors as 1-cells and natural transformations as 2-cells.

concepts.title: Enriched categories preview
concepts.blurb: Enrichment replaces Hom-sets by objects in a monoidal $\mathcal{V}$; Ab-enriched gives additive categories, and Lawvere showed a metric space is exactly a $([0,\infty],+,0)$-enriched category.
